## Post Details

### X — 08:30 BKK (01:30 UTC)
**ID:** `blaze-daily-2026-05-15-x1-*`
**Account:** Twitter / @jeditrinupab (account 16969)
**Source theme:** LF1 — AI Operating System / DeployCo

> Most businesses gave every person a ChatGPT login and called it an AI strategy.
>
> That's not an AI strategy.
>
> That's hoping the smartest people figure it out on their own.
>
> Old model: each person uses AI their way.
> New model: the business designs the workflow.
>
> The difference shows up in revenue, not demos.

**Voice anchors:** Old way vs New way, systems over superheroes, revenue visibility

---

### LinkedIn — 10:00 BKK (03:00 UTC)
**ID:** `blaze-daily-2026-05-15-li1-*`
**Account:** LinkedIn / Trinupab Jiratritarn (account 19173)
**Source theme:** LF1 — Founder AI Operating System / 10-80-10 rule

> Most founders ask the wrong question about AI.
>
> Not: "Which tool should we try?"
>
> But: "Which workflows should we redesign first?"
>
> Here's the model I use — the 10-80-10 rule:
>
> • 10% — the founder or team lead sets the direction and quality bar
> • 80% — AI does the heavy work: drafting, summarizing, sorting, preparing
> • 10% — a human reviews, approves, and takes the action
>
> It works for proposals, lead qualification, content, meeting summaries, financial reports.
>
> The businesses that will win are not the ones with the most subscriptions.
>
> They are the ones that sat down and redesigned which decisions still need a human, and which ones can be prepared by the system.
>
> That redesign is the real work.

**Voice anchors:** Jet POV / founder belief, operational framework, systems over superheroes

---

### X — 12:30 BKK (05:30 UTC)
**ID:** `blaze-daily-2026-05-15-x2-*`
**Account:** Twitter / @jeditrinupab (account 16969)
**Source theme:** LF2 — 35+ ChatGPT adoption / AI-readable business

> Your 35+ customers are using ChatGPT before they contact your team.
>
> They're researching your pricing.
> Comparing alternatives.
> Preparing objections.
>
> They arrive more informed than before.
>
> Old sales motion: explain everything.
> New sales motion: start from where the informed buyer already is.
>
> If your FAQ, comparison page, and sales pitch are still vague — you're slower than your own customer.

**Voice anchors:** Old way vs New way, Thai SME operator context, clear next step implied

---

### X — 20:30 BKK (13:30 UTC)
**ID:** `blaze-daily-2026-05-15-x3-*`
**Account:** Twitter / @jeditrinupab (account 16969)
**Source theme:** LF3 — AI ad brief / Google Small Brief

> Most ad spend problems are actually brief problems.
>
> The message isn't sharp.
> The customer isn't specific.
> The offer isn't clear.
>
> Boosting a vague post just means more people see something forgettable.
>
> Before you buy media:
>
> -> Use AI to generate 5 campaign angles
> -> Ask it to play devil's advocate on each one
> -> Pick the angle a 45-year-old business owner understands in 10 seconds
>
> Then spend.

**Voice anchors:** Thai SME marketing context, specific operational next step, clear "do this" framing
